errata.title_quirk: PDF title says 'Ensemble E2, E4, E8 Errata' but the scope section names E4, E6, E8 -- 'E2' is a transcription error in the source PDF title.

items.title: Clock pre-scale divider for RTC is incorrectly reset
items.consequence: POR_N assertion or STOP-mode wake resets the RTC clock pre-scaler divider while VDD_VBATT is sufficient.  RTC reverts to its default 32 kHz rate; the firmware-configured rate (typically 1 Hz) is lost.  Firmware can re-write the divider but the elapsed-time accuracy is gone.

items.title: RTC time drifts while POR_N reset signal is asserted
items.resolution: No fix planned for future silicon revision
items.consequence: While POR_N is asserted, the RTC clock source switches from the high-accuracy 32 kHz LFXO to the lower-accuracy LFRC (~5 % offset).  After de-assertion the source switches back to LFXO.  RTC accuracy drifts proportional to total POR_N hold time -- a 20 s POR_N assertion incurs ~1 s of RTC error.
items.workaround: Use an external real-time clock source if high accuracy is required

items.title: STOP mode current is higher than specification
items.consequence: Current entering VDD_BATT at 3.3 V in STOP mode is approximately 2 uA higher than the value specified in Table 5-5 of the E4 / E6 / E8 datasheets.  Reduces VDD_BATT battery life.
items.workaround: Use silicon revision A1, STOP mode current is as specified in the datasheet

items.title: External reset supervisor is required in some situations
items.resolution: No fix planned for future silicon revision
items.consequence: During the initial 10 ms cold-boot ramp at VDD_MAIN + VDD_BUCK, the supply voltage must rise monotonically and must never dip below 1.65 V once 1.65 V is reached.  Violations can permanently damage the SoC.
items.workaround: Use an external reset supervisor that drives POR_N active (low) while the supply is below 1.65 V.  See Alif Application Note AAPN0036 (PCB Layout Guidelines for Ensemble E4 / E6 / E8) for the reference circuit.

notes: Datasheet v1.0 released (Alif_E4_Datasheet_v1.0.pdf, 2026-05-11).  Peripheral counts are minimally populated (`usb_2` only) -- the v1.0 datasheet ships but the SDK's full per-peripheral count ingest hasn't run against it yet; do NOT fall back to E8's counts blindly because E8 ships HexSPI while E4 ships OctalSPI per the ALP module table, so the IO mix differs.  Re-ingest tracked separately.
Topology: 2× M55 (no A32), 2× U55 + 1× U85, ISP/JPEG, 5.5 MB MRAM, up to 9.75 MB SRAM, 2× OSPI.  Confirmed by the v1.0 datasheet.
HWRM v0.3 is Alif-confidential -- referenced here for traceability; the file itself stays out of git (the `confidential: true` flag on the hwrm block makes that explicit).
Errata AERR0012 v2.0 covers E4/E6/E8 jointly; individual `items` list is pending ingestion against the errata doc.
Order code AE402FA0E5597LE0 backs the E1M-AEN401 module SKU.
USB = xHCI USB-2.0 dual-role @ 0x48200000 (IRQ 101), 16 endpoints; grounded from the Alif DFP soc.h/soc_features.h (AE402FA0E5597). Host mode supported (E4 datasheet 'USB 2.0 HS/FS Host/Device').
